heading: Definitions
label: 4210 Definitions

children.heading: Agreement on Agriculture
children.label: 1 Agreement on Agriculture
children.text: The term “Agreement on Agriculture” means the agreement referred to in .section 3511(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Agreement on Safeguards
children.label: 2 Agreement on Safeguards
children.text: The term “Agreement on Safeguards” means the agreement referred to in .section 3511(d)(13) of this title

children.heading: Agreement on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures
children.label: 3 Agreement on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures
children.text: The term “Agreement on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures” means the agreement referred to in .section 3511(d)(12) of this title

children.heading: Antidumping Agreement
children.label: 4 Antidumping Agreement
children.text: The term “Antidumping Agreement” means the Agreement on Implementation of Article VI of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade 1994 referred to in .section 3511(d)(7) of this title

children.heading: Appellate Body
children.label: 5 Appellate Body
children.text: The term “Appellate Body” means the Appellate Body established under Article 17.1 of the Dispute Settlement Understanding.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “common multilateral environmental agreement” means any agreement specified in subparagraph (B) or included under subparagraph (C) to which both the United States and one or more other parties to the negotiations are full parties, including any current or future mutually agreed upon protocols, amendments, annexes, or adjustments to such an agreement.

children.label: i
children.text: The Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora, done at Washington (27 UST 1087; TIAS 8249).March 3, 1973

children.label: ii
children.text: The Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer, done at Montreal .September 16, 1987

children.label: iii
children.text: The Protocol of 1978 Relating to the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships, 1973, done at London .February 17, 1978

children.label: iv
children.text: The Convention on Wetlands of International Importance Especially as Waterfowl Habitat, done at Ramsar (TIAS 11084).February 2, 1971

children.label: v
children.text: The Convention on the Conservation of Antarctic Marine Living Resources, done at Canberra (33 UST 3476).May 20, 1980

children.label: vi
children.text: The International Convention for the Regulation of Whaling, done at Washington ().December 2, 194662 Stat. 1716

children.label: vii
children.text: The Convention for the Establishment of an Inter-American Tropical Tuna Commission, done at Washington (1 UST 230).May 31, 1949

children.heading: Additional agreements
children.label: C Additional agreements
children.text: Both the United States and one or more other parties to the negotiations may agree to include any other multilateral environmental or conservation agreement to which they are full parties as a common multilateral environmental agreement under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: freedom of association;

children.label: B
children.text: the effective recognition of the right to collective bargaining;

children.label: C
children.text: the elimination of all forms of forced or compulsory labor;

children.label: D
children.text: the effective abolition of child labor and a prohibition on the worst forms of child labor; and

children.label: E
children.text: the elimination of discrimination in respect of employment and occupation.

children.heading: Dispute Settlement Understanding
children.label: 8 Dispute Settlement Understanding
children.text: The term “Dispute Settlement Understanding” means the Understanding on Rules and Procedures Governing the Settlement of Disputes referred to in .section 3511(d)(16) of this title

children.heading: Enabling Clause
children.label: 9 Enabling Clause
children.text: The term “Enabling Clause” means the Decision on Differential and More Favourable Treatment, Reciprocity and Fuller Participation of Developing Countries (L/4903), adopted , under GATT 1947 (as defined in ).November 28, 1979section 3501 of this title

children.heading: Environmental laws
children.label: 10 Environmental laws
children.text: The term “environmental laws”, with respect to the laws of the United States, means environmental statutes and regulations enforceable by action of the Federal Government.

children.heading: GATT 1994
children.label: 11 GATT 1994
children.text: The term “GATT 1994” has the meaning given that term in .section 3501 of this title

children.heading: General Agreement on Trade in Services
children.label: 12 General Agreement on Trade in Services
children.text: The term “General Agreement on Trade in Services” means the General Agreement on Trade in Services (referred to in ).section 3511(d)(14) of this title

children.heading: Government Procurement Agreement
children.label: 13 Government Procurement Agreement
children.text: The term “Government Procurement Agreement” means the Agreement on Government Procurement referred to in .section 3511(d)(17) of this title

children.heading: ILO
children.label: 14 ILO
children.text: The term “ILO” means the International Labor Organization.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to which, as a result of the Uruguay Round Agreements, the rate of duty was the subject of tariff reductions by the United States and, pursuant to such Agreements, was reduced on , to a rate that was not less than 97.5 percent of the rate of duty that applied to such article on ; orJanuary 1, 1995December 31, 1994

children.label: B
children.text: which was subject to a tariff rate quota on .June 29, 2015

children.heading: Information Technology Agreement
children.label: 16 Information Technology Agreement
children.text: The term “Information Technology Agreement” means the Ministerial Declaration on Trade in Information Technology Products of the World Trade Organization, agreed to at Singapore .December 13, 1996

children.heading: Internationally recognized core labor standards
children.label: 17 Internationally recognized core labor standards
children.text: The term “internationally recognized core labor standards” means the core labor standards only as stated in the ILO Declaration on Fundamental Principles and Rights at Work and its Follow-Up (1998).

children.heading: Labor laws
children.label: 18 Labor laws
children.text: The term “labor laws” means the statutes and regulations, or provisions thereof, of a party to the negotiations that are directly related to core labor standards as well as other labor protections for children and minors and acceptable conditions of work with respect to minimum wages, hours of work, and occupational safety and health, and for the United States, includes Federal statutes and regulations addressing those standards, protections, or conditions, but does not include State or local labor laws.

children.label: A
children.text: a United States citizen;

children.label: B
children.text: a partnership, corporation, or other legal entity that is organized under the laws of the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: a partnership, corporation, or other legal entity that is organized under the laws of a foreign country and is controlled by entities described in subparagraph (B) or United States citizens, or both.

children.heading: Uruguay Round Agreements
children.label: 20 Uruguay Round Agreements
children.text: The term “Uruguay Round Agreements” has the meaning given that term in .section 3501(7) of this title

children.heading: World Trade Organization; WTO
children.label: 21 World Trade Organization; WTO
children.text: The terms “World Trade Organization” and “WTO” mean the organization established pursuant to the WTO Agreement.

children.heading: WTO Agreement
children.label: 22 WTO Agreement
children.text: The term “WTO Agreement” means the Agreement Establishing the World Trade Organization entered into on .April 15, 1994

children.heading: WTO member
children.label: 23 WTO member
children.text: The term “WTO member” has the meaning given that term in .section 3501(10) of this title
